description: Dynamic bridge — 2026-04-28 (Tuesday) session / Week of Apr 28 - May 2

market_regime.label: GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED_OVERSOLD_WEEK_BEGINS
market_regime.key_risk: Brent $105.33 (Apr 25 settle) — sustained above $100 for 5th session. Hormuz blockade active. FII net sold ₹17,140 cr week of Apr 20-24. Nifty 4-day consecutive decline (-2.78% Apr 22-24 alone). IT sector cascade (INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% Apr 24) not yet digested.
market_regime.dominant_risk: Multi-binary confluence: (1) Israel-Iran weekend binary (any strike → Brent $112+, Monday gap-down -1.5% to -2%); (2) Continuing IT sector earnings cascade (WIPRO Q4 due); (3) FII selling 5th week risk. Below 23,800 Nifty = algo stop territory → 23,500-23,600 opens up.
market_regime.crude_note: Brent $105.33 Apr 25 settle — rebounded from Apr 24's $100.71 intraday low. Back above $105 psychological level. Sustained $100-108 zone. Regime change requires <$95 sustained 5 sessions.
market_regime.regime_shift_note: BankNifty broke 56,000 critical support Apr 24 (close 55,956.85). IT sector in cascade. Defense bid sustained (COCHINSHIP holding ₹1,638). Weekend binary still unresolved Monday morning — check Israel-Iran headlines FIRST before any session assessment.

key_drivers: Friday close 23,897.95 (-1.14%) below psychological 24,000 — pressure persists
Weekend BINARY event: Israel strike threats vs Iran + Hormuz shipping disruption still active

Brent closed $100.71 (-4.15% intraday) — cooled but remains in elevated $100-108 zone. Weekend spike to $108+ on any fresh headline = -0.5% to -1.0% gap down; pullback <$95 = flat to mildly positive open
FII selling 4th consecutive day (~₹3,254 cr Apr 24) — structural flow overhang
IT sector: INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% Friday. Monday cash market likely to gap down further in IT unless weekend tech guidance improves
Defense basket firm (COCHINSHIP +4.51%) — expect further geopolitical premium bid if weekend headlines adverse
US Fri session + SGX/Nikkei Monday open will shape overnight sentiment — check GIFT Nifty 06:00 IST Monday
opening_bias.rationale_note: Confidence 2/5 because weekend binary risk (Israel-Iran escalation) can swing open ±1% in either direction. Base case leans mildly negative on continued FII outflow + below-psychological close. Any ceasefire re-affirmation overnight could flip to gap-up.

session_direction.key_assumption: Oversold bounce sustains after 4-day cumulative -2.78% decline. DII support firm at 23,800-23,900 (already tested intraday low 23,936). Brent cooled to $99-100 zone reduces import-cost overhang. IT sector stabilization at lows without fresh negative catalyst (HCLTECH, INFY consolidating). HDFC Bank Q4 already digested (NII +3.2% YoY, NIM 3.38% — modest beat on profit, margin compression a yellow flag for BankNifty). Mid-session short-covering capped at 24,150-24,200 by FII overhang and unresolved Israel-Iran weekend binary still latent. Base case: Nifty closes 24,000-24,150 (+0.4 to +1.0% from open). DII-driven recovery, not structural shift.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $98-103 — base case Brent holds in this band as Iran talks halted but no fresh strike. Break <$95 sustained 2 sessions = constructive structural shift; break >$105 on fresh headline = afternoon reversal risk.

scenarios.name: CONTINUATION_BOUNCE (50%)
scenarios.description: Oversold bounce sustains. DII buying defends 23,940 morning low. Short-covering builds through morning. IT/Banking stabilize. Brent holds $99-101. Closes 24,000-24,150.

scenarios.name: INTRADAY_FADE (30%)
scenarios.description: Morning bounce fades by afternoon as FII selling resumes (5th day risk). HDFC Bank margin compression (3.38% NIM) weighs on banking. IT no recovery. Closes 23,940-24,030 (mild gain or flat).

scenarios.name: ESCALATION_REVERSAL (15%)
scenarios.description: Fresh Iran-Israel headline drops mid-session. Brent re-spikes >$103. Defense ripper, banking/IT smashed. Nifty reverses to red close 23,800-23,940.

scenarios.name: RIPPING_OVERSOLD_BOUNCE (5%)
scenarios.description: Strong short-covering compounds with DII buying. FII covers part of week net short. Brent stays cool. Nifty rips to 24,200+. P031 (24,050-24,300 by May 1) suddenly back in play.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Brent re-accelerated to $105 Apr 25 (from $100.71 Apr 24) — suggests market is pricing in weekend risk. Collapse probability raised from 40% to 45% given: Iran refused next-round talks, Vance cancelled Islamabad, Israel defense minister explicitly seeking 'green light' for strikes. Stalemate (no deal, no strike) is the narrow middle path.

stocks_to_watch: HCLTECH: ₹1,203.20 — P028 scores Apr 28 (EXPECTED RIGHT). Watch for any IT recovery signals; M013 cap prevents bullish outlook.
IRFC: ₹103.36 — P010 scores Apr 29 (EXPECTED WRONG). Monitor budget capex announcement as only re-rating catalyst. Avg ₹130 = -20.5% loss position.
ASTRA MICRO: ₹1,123.75 — P030 scores Apr 30 (ABOVE_RANGE). M012 v5 floor at ₹1,050 (P036). Any drop to ₹1,080-1,100 = buy signal if Brent stays $100+.
COCHINSHIP: ₹1,638.40 — P033 target ₹1,720-1,800 by May 1. Watch for Monday defense basket follow-through if weekend headlines escalatory.
SETFGOLD: ₹127.73 — P034 range ₹125-130 by May 6. Haven bid durability check. Any Brent spike >$108 = SETFGOLD spike >₹130 (P034 ceiling breach).
MRPL: ₹186.38 — P035 target ₹190-200 by May 25. Watch: Brent $105 is at upper P035 tolerance — if sustained $108+, P035 auto-invalidation triggers.
COAL INDIA: ~₹432 — P037 target ₹448-470 by May 15. Monitor coal-crude substitution data. Strong hold if Brent stays $100+.
BANKNIFTY: 55,956.85 — HDFC Bank Q4 (Apr 28-29) key catalyst. P032 (>56,500 by May 1) + P038 (fails 57,000 by May 5) create near-term resolution window.
BankNifty 56,000 support: Must watch — broke Apr 24. Reclaim 56,500 = P032 trajectory. Stay below 55,500 = P038 confirmation.
SWIGGY: ~₹287 — P040 target ₹265-278 by May 20. Brent $105 bearish confirmation needed. Watch Zomato Q4 (sector rerate risk).
prompt_for_morning_brief: MORNING BRIEF 2026-04-28 (Tuesday): (1) Score P028 HCLTECH: close Apr 28 vs target <₹1,430. EXPECTED RIGHT (at ₹1,203). (2) CRITICAL CHECK: Did HDFC Bank Q4FY26 results drop? If yes — beat/miss vs consensus? BankNifty direction? P032 check (>56,500) scoring window Apr 28-May 1. (3) Weekend geopolitical: Israel-Iran status, Hormuz blockade update, Brent overnight. Was P031 Monday gap-down direction correct? (4) Regime check: BankNifty — did 56,000 support reclaim or further breakdown? P038 (fails 57,000) tracking vs P032 (>56,500) tension. (5) IT sector: HCLTECH/INFY Monday session? Any WIPRO pre-announcement? P039 (Nifty IT underperforms 3-5% by May 8) tracking check. (6) ASTRA MICRO — Monday open. P030 (1010-1060 Apr 30) is above range; P036 (>1050 May 5) is framework-aligned floor. Confirm M012 v5 holding. (7) Regime tag for Monday: GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED_OVERSOLD_WEEK_BEGINS. Update based on actual Monday open + HDFC Bank result.

key_events_this_week.event: P028 HCLTECH check + HDFC Bank Q4FY26 results (expected)
key_events_this_week.impact: HIGH — P028 expected RIGHT (HCLTECH ₹1,203 vs <₹1,430). HDFC Bank beat could catalyze BankNifty P032 recovery toward 56,500.

key_events_this_week.event: P010 IRFC check + Axis Bank Q4 (possible)
key_events_this_week.impact: MEDIUM — P010 expected WRONG. Axis Bank beat would add to BankNifty recovery narrative.

key_events_this_week.event: P030 ASTRA MICRO check + WIPRO Q4FY26 results (possible)
key_events_this_week.impact: HIGH — P030 expected ABOVE_RANGE. WIPRO results = SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT 6th occurrence test.
CRITICAL — Triple check day. Week direction determines 3 scores simultaneously. Defense (P033) requires escalation; index (P031/P032) requires quiet weekend + recovery.

active_promoted_patterns: M012 v5 STRENGTHENED (Defense Sector Duration Premium — requires Brent <$92 + Hormuz open 5D for unwind)
M013 (IT Regime Risk Filter — cap IT confidence at 2★ in active geopolitical regime)
FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger — 25-30% probability within 72h of talks failure)
SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT (5 confirmations — no post-earnings IT breakout predictions)
TALKS_FAILED_WAR_PREMIUM_RETURN PROMOTED v6 (2 confirmations — 1-2 day swing pattern ONLY)
observing_patterns: TALKS_FAILED_WAR_PREMIUM_RETURN (AMENDED — 1-2 day swing pattern only, not 7D+)

observing_patterns.description: Dual adverse binary (earnings + geopolitical) within 5 sessions → multiplicative Nifty impact.
Haven bid persists 2-5 sessions past trigger resolution.
Consumer disc benefits from Brent cooling over 2W windows.
IT sector cascade (INFY + HCLTECH) overrides Brent cooling thesis on Nifty direction.

portfolio_summary.note: Estimated values — pending official mark-to-market. Nifty +1.26% week, defense/renewables outperformed. Key drivers: IRCON +8.65%, SUZLON +5.33%, DMART +4.52%, MRPL +4.69% on Friday.
portfolio_summary.biggest_position_risks: IRFC (₹105 vs ₹130 avg = -19.2% loss, 200 shares), SWIGGY (₹271 vs ₹299 avg = -9.3% loss), MRPL (₹183 vs ₹196 avg = -6.8% loss)
portfolio_summary.biggest_winners: ASTRA MICRO (₹1,130 est vs ₹562 avg = +101% gain), SUZLON (₹53 vs ₹41 avg = +30.6%), COAL INDIA (₹432 vs ₹346 avg = +24.9%)

scoring_queue.note: At ₹1,203.20 (16% below ₹1,430). M013 + SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT (4th occurrence). Would be first RIGHT prediction since P019 (Apr 15) — breaks 9-prediction cold streak.
At ₹103.36 (8.8 pts above ceiling of ₹95). PSU re-rating thesis failed (ceasefire rally overrode). Portfolio position: avg ₹130.01 = -20.5% loss.
At ₹1,123.75 (6% above ₹1,060 ceiling). M012 v5 prevents defense unwind. Will score ABOVE_RANGE. P036 replaces with M012-aligned floor prediction (>₹1,050).

scoring_queue.status: UNCERTAIN — below target range by 152-402 pts
scoring_queue.note: Requires +0.65% to +1.69% recovery from Apr 24 close. Base case (40% OVERSOLD_BOUNCE) makes this feasible by May 1. Escalation scenario (15%) invalidates. VOLATILE_SIDEWAYS week creates path.

Needs +543pts from ₹55,956. HDFC Bank Q4 beat (Apr 28-29) could catalyze BankNifty bounce. P038 (new) caps at 57,000 — creates tension with P032 target. Resolution depends on HDFC Bank result quality.

At ₹1,638.40 (-2% from P031 Apr 24 context price). +5-9.9% needed in 5 sessions. M012 v5 provides defense floor; geopolitical escalation could drive the target. High-conviction defense play if weekend headlines adverse.

opening_bias_actual.gift_nifty_note: GIFT Nifty pre-open Apr 27 ~23,920-23,940 zone (estimated from open). GIFT signaled flat-to-mildly-positive open vs. forecast 23,840 estimate (-0.45% miss to upside).
opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_MILD_GAP_DOWN (-0.25%, GIFT est 23,840). Actual gap +0.199% (open 23945.45 vs prev close 23897.95) — GAP_UP direction OPPOSITE of forecast. Override condition partially triggered: Brent cooled below $100 (WTI above $96 = Brent ~$99-100), enabling oversold bounce + DII activation. The override scenario predicted +0.5 to +1.0% gap up but actual was milder +0.20% — markets cautious due to lingering Israel-Iran weekend tensions (Trump halted talks, Iran FM left Pakistan without meeting US). Graded WRONG — direction opposite, magnitude smaller than override scenario. Missed signal: framework correctly mapped override conditions (Brent cooling) but base case underweighted the oversold-bounce probability after 4-day decline (cumulative -2.78%).

overnight_delta_vs_forecast.ceasefire_status: STALEMATE — Trump halted negotiations; Iran FM left Pakistan without meeting US officials Sunday; no deal, no strike confirmed yet
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.hormuz_status: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed; tensions persist but no fresh strike
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.brent_vs_eod: ~-$1 to -$2 vs Apr 25 settle ($105.33). Brent cooled to ~$99-100 zone overnight despite stalled talks — divergence from war-premium thesis.
overnight_delta_vs_forecast.net_effect: Weekend passed without escalation strike. Brent cooled despite stalled talks (positioning unwind / supply expectations). Combined with 4-day oversold conditions, drove modest gap-up open. FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger) candidate did not fire — talks halted not restarted.

session_direction_accuracy_today.direction_note: Forecast BEARISH_VOLATILE (morning-brief revision, -0.6%, range 23,900-24,220). Actual: Nifty closed 23,897.95 (-1.14% from 24,173.05). Direction CORRECT (bearish confirmed). Close 23,897.95 is 2 pts BELOW range low (23,900) — essentially AT the forecast range edge. Magnitude ~1.9x stronger than -0.6% base case; actual fell into the ESCALATION_SPIRAL scenario range (23,850-23,950, 15% prob). NOTE: Escalation scenario trigger (Brent >$110) did NOT fire — Brent actually cooled -4.15% intraday to $100.71. Driver was IT sector cascade (INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% follow-through) + weekend risk-off positioning, not geopolitical re-escalation.
session_direction_accuracy_today.range_note: Close 23,897.95 vs forecast range 23,900-24,220. 2.05 pts below range low — ESSENTIALLY AT edge. Counts as marginal range breach (within 0.01%).
session_direction_accuracy_today.magnitude_note: Forecast -0.6% base, scenarios spanned -0.3% to -1.3%. Actual -1.14% falls in escalation-spiral scenario but exceeded base case by ~0.54pts.
session_direction_accuracy_today.brent_note: Forecast Brent $103-108 sustained. Actual $100.71 (-4.15% intraday). Brent forecast WRONG on direction — cooled rather than stayed elevated. Yet Nifty still sold off — key insight: session was dominated by IT sector cascade, not crude macro. FORECAST_PAT007 candidate: idiosyncratic sector drag overrides macro thesis.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scoring_note: Direction CORRECT (bearish). Close marginally below range low (2 pts, 0.01%). Magnitude ~1.9x base case but within scenario envelope. Brent forecast WRONG on direction. Overall PARTIAL_CORRECT — the session-direction call held but macro driver attribution was off.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scenarios_hit: ESCALATION_SPIRAL range (23,850-23,950) — close 23,897.95 sits squarely inside. Scenario was assigned 15% prob; reality triggered via IT cascade rather than Brent spike (different mechanism, same price path).

portfolio_snapshot.note: Estimated values as of Apr 24 (last trading day). Week was brutal: Nifty -1.87%, FII sold ₹17,140 cr. IT cascade hurt broader sentiment.
portfolio_snapshot.biggest_position_risks: IRFC (₹103 vs ₹130 avg = -20.5% loss), SWIGGY (₹287 vs ₹299 avg = -4.0% loss), MRPL (₹186 vs ₹196 avg = -5.1% loss)
portfolio_snapshot.biggest_winners: ASTRA MICRO (₹1,124 est vs ₹562 avg = +99.9% gain), SUZLON (~₹52-53 vs ₹40.55 avg = +29-31%), COAL INDIA (~₹432 vs ₹346 avg = +24.9%)
portfolio_snapshot.watch_this_week: HDFC Bank Q4 (Apr 28-29) — if portfolio has HDFC Bank exposure, key catalyst. Coal India P037 target ₹448-470 first test next 3 weeks.
